summary: P2-4-003 hook-gate sweep baseline was implemented, documented, and verified by automated tests.
non_blocking_issues: The current diagnostic boundary is torque-dominated for local_hook_scale values 1 and 8; it should not be interpreted as a final physical optimum for hook stiffness.

The failing high-torque condition can also deform body shape by the final step, so hook/body interpretation uses first_fail_category_nonbody priority and retains body_shape_pass as a parallel diagnostic.
tests_reviewed: uv run pytest tests/test_simulation.py -k "phaseb1 or phaseb2"
commit hook: uv run ruff format --check .
commit hook: uv run ruff check .
adr_reason: No significant architecture, dependency, output-format, or physical-model change was introduced; the work standardizes an existing sweep helper option and documents current hook-gate diagnostics.
next_actions: Proceed to P2-5-004 body+hook+single flagellum motor-on stability.
After PR #46 is merged, retarget or rebase this stacked branch onto main.
